nameMeanings.name: (wife
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Wynn  .
Americanized form of German   Wein  .

variants.name: Wyne
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Wine   (see   Winn  ).
Dutch: variant, archaic or Americanized, of Wijne, itself a variant of Winne .

variants.name: Wines
meanings: English (Somerset): variant of Wine , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
History: Barnabas Wines came from Wales to Watertown, MA, in or before 1635.

variants.name: Wynn
meanings: English: from a Middle English survival of the Old English word wine ‘friend’. There was also an Old English personal name Wine, derived from this word, which may have given rise to the surname. It is supported by the variety of surnames that evidently originated in patronymic or diminutive forms of this name, such as Winson .
Welsh: from gwyn ‘fair, white’, also used as a personal name. See also Gwin .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Wilhelm
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Fritz, Hans, Heinz, Ewald, Gerhard, Helmut, Markus, Ralf, Rudie, Balzer.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Willahalm, composed of the elements wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’.

variants.name: Wellens
meanings: Flemish, Dutch, and German: patronymic from Welle(n), a vernacular form of the personal name Willem (see Wilhelm ) and reportedly also of Walter .

variants.name: Wilhelms
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto.
German: patronymic from   Wilhelm  .

variants.name: Willeke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Klaus.
North German: from a pet form of the personal name   Wilhelm  .

variants.name: Wilharm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Udo.
German: variant of   Wilhelm  .

variants.name: Wilham
meanings: German: probably a variant of   Wilhelm  .

variants.name: Wilken
meanings: North German: patronymic from a Low German pet form of   Wilhelm  .
Scottish (Aberdeenshire): variant of   Wilkin  .

variants.name: Wilhelmi
meanings: German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Wilhelm  , using the Latin genitive.

variants.name: Wilhelmsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Alf, Erik.  German Hans, Kurt, Bredt.
Norwegian, Danish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name   Wilhelm  . Compare   Wilhelmson   and   Williamsen  .

variants.name: Wilhelmy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jochen, Viktor, Xaver.
German: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Wilhelm  , a variant of   Wilhelmi  .

variants.name: Wilke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Arno, Bernd, Gerhard, Siegfried, Wilfried, Wilhelm, Wolfgang, Angelika.
North German and Dutch: from a Low German pet form of   Wilhelm  .
English (Lancashire): variant of   Wilk  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.
